job.title: Delivery Manager | Offshore | India
job.description: We are looking for a results-driven IT Delivery Manager to oversee the end-to-end delivery of technology solutions across multiple projects and clients. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in IT project management, stakeholder coordination, and Agile/Waterfall methodologies, ensuring high-quality delivery within timelines and budget. Key Responsibilities: Manage end-to-end delivery of IT projects across multiple teams and geographies Collaborate with clients, business stakeholders, and technical teams to define project scope, timelines, and success metrics Ensure adherence to SLAs, budgets, and quality standards Identify and mitigate delivery risks and dependencies Lead and mentor project teams to ensure high performance and continuous improvement Track progress through regular reporting and communication with leadership Required Skills: Proven experience in IT project/delivery management Strong understanding of software development life cycle (SDLC) Proficiency in project management tools (JIRA, MS Project, etc.) Excellent communication, leadership, and stakeholder management skills Familiarity with Agile, Scrum, and Waterfall delivery models Preferred Qualifications: PMP / Prince2 / Scrum Master certification Experience managing enterprise-level or client-facing delivery projects Background in application development or infrastructure projects Originally posted on Himalayas

match_snapshot.ai_reasoning: The candidate's current role as Delivery Manager at NIIT Limited maps almost directly to this position, with demonstrated experience managing end-to-end IT project delivery across multiple clients (PwC, MetLife), Agile/Scrum methodologies, stakeholder coordination, risk mitigation, and team mentoring. Their 5+ year track record on the MetLife DAP project and ongoing PwC engagement shows sustained multi-client delivery management. match_snapshot.bridge_advice: Highlight specific SLA adherence metrics, team size managed, and budget figures from the PwC and MetLife engagements to demonstrate the quantitative delivery accountability Photon expects.

job.title: Learning and Development Specialist
job.description: GR8 Tech builds B2B iGaming platforms for operators who play to lead. We deliver full-cycle, high-impact tech designed to scale — from seamless integrations and expert consulting to long-term operational support. Our platform powers millions of active players and drives real business growth. Call it what it is: the iGaming Platform for Champions. With 1000+ GR8 people across locations and time zones, we don’t just ship technology — we help operators build success stories across brands, markets, and geos. Our ambition drives us. Our people make it real. If you’re a challenger in spirit and a champion in action — join us. Why this role exists: This role exists to execute and operationally manage learning and development initiatives. It supports the design, delivery, and evaluation of learning programs to ensure their quality and promote a continuous development culture. The role collaborates with internal stakeholders and external providers to ensure effective and timely delivery of learning initiatives and supports ongoing program improvements. What you’ll drive: L&D strategy execution & learning process support Support the execution of L&D strategies aligned with business and employee needs. Assist in implementing learning processes within the organization. Receive and manage training requests to support continuous learning culture. Learning solutions delivery & program coordination Assist in the design and delivery of learning solutions and training content aligned with business objectives. Coordinate training events including scheduling, materials preparation, online venue management, and speaker coordination. Maintain records of learning programs including attendance, progress tracking, and assessments. Stakeholder coordination & communication Assist in engaging employees, managers, and HR to deliver learning solutions. Support communication and guide employees in navigating learning solutions to ensure high participation. Support administration of contracts and relationships with external vendors and training providers. Operational support & program improvement Collect and analyze participant feedback to evaluate program success and suggest improvements. Assist in implementing changes to learning initiatives based on evaluations, business needs, and trends. Monitor and execute defined L&D KPIs to ensure process quality. What makes you a GR8 fit: Must-have 2+ years of experience in L&D Specialist, L&D Project Manager, or Event Manager roles. Proven experience in implementing talent development initiatives and delivering L&D projects. Understanding of adult learning principles and instructional design basics. Experience in developing and managing training and development programs. Familiarity with learning management systems and educational technology tools. Ability to manage multiple tasks and prioritize effectively to meet deadlines. Ability to identify problems and risks and suggest solutions. Ability to collect, analyze, and report on training effectiveness metrics and feedback. Readiness to generate and interpret basic reports and dashboards. Strong written and verbal communication skills. Ability to collaborate with stakeholders including employees, managers, and external vendors. Nice-to-have Experience in building and implementing L&D practices for remote teams. Why you’ll love working here: Benefits Cafeteria — annual budget you allocate to: Sports • Medical • Mental health • Home office • Languages. Work-life & support Paid maternity/paternity leave + monthly childcare allowance. 20+ vacation days, unlimited sick leave, emergency time off. Remote-first + tech support + coworking compensation. Team events (online/offline/offsite). Learning culture with internal courses + growth programs. Our culture & core values: GR8 Tech culture is how we win — through trust, ownership, and a growth mindset. We move fast, stay curious, and keep it real, with open feedback, room to experiment, and a team that’s got your back. FUELLED BY TRUST : we’re open, honest, and have each other’s backs. OWN YOUR GAME : we take initiative and own what we do. ACCELER8 : we move fast, focus smart, and keep it simple. CHALLENGE ACCEPTED : we grow through challenges and stay curious. BULLETPROOF : we’re resilient, ready, and always have a plan. Originally posted on Himalayas

match_snapshot.ai_reasoning: The GR8 Tech L&D Specialist role closely matches Anil's core expertise in executing L&D strategies, collaborating with stakeholders, managing vendors, and ensuring quality of learning programs. His 18+ years at Thomson Reuters and experience with training needs analysis, program delivery, and external provider management are directly applicable. The primary gap is that this is a specialist/executor role — potentially a step down from his Director-level title — and the iGaming domain is unfamiliar.
match_snapshot.bridge_advice: Position yourself as a senior practitioner who can immediately own end-to-end program quality and vendor management, and acknowledge the iGaming context as a domain-agnostic instructional design strength.
match_reasons: 18 yrs blended learning design and L&D program management expertise
Vendor management and external provider coordination at Thomson Reuters

missing_skills: Senior-level L&D strategy ownership (not just support)

job.title: Operations Training Specialist, Contact Center
job.description: Sidecar Health is redefining health insurance. Our mission is to make excellent healthcare affordable and accessible for everyone. We know that to accomplish this lofty mission, we need driven people who will make things happen. The passionate people who make up Sidecar Health ’s team come from all over, with backgrounds as tech leaders, policy makers, healthcare professionals, and beyond. And they all have one thing in common—the desire to fix a broken system and make it more personalized, affordable, and transparent. If you want to use your talents to transform healthcare in the United States, come join us! About the Role As an Operations Training Specialist on our Enablement team, you will own the full training lifecycle for Sidecar Health 's operations teams — from building programs to running them to keeping them current and effective over time. This is a hands-on role in every sense: you design the content, facilitate the sessions, manage the logistics, and follow through on outcomes from a new hire's first day through the point they are ready to perform independently on the floor. Your primary focus will be supporting Member Care operations, with the scope and flexibility to contribute to additional operations teams as the business grows. You will work closely with stakeholders across Member Care, Claims, Clinical, and Provider Ops, and you will be an active contributor to a broader enablement function that spans training program management, knowledge management, and ongoing learning and development. Your contributions will directly shape the readiness and confidence of our operations teams, equipping them with the tools, knowledge, and skills needed to deliver an outstanding member experience. What You'll Do: Own the end-to-end new hire onboarding experience for assigned operations teams — program design, content creation, classroom delivery, nesting coordination, job shadowing, and floor readiness evaluation Design and facilitate ongoing refresher, upskilling, and process change training as business needs evolve Build and maintain all training materials for assigned teams — e-learning modules, facilitator guides, presentations, knowledge checks, role-play scenarios, and assessments Own operations-specific knowledge content within the knowledge management system, ensuring accuracy and alignment with current policies, workflows, and product changes Assess the training and knowledge impact of operational and product changes as they come through, and proactively develop or update materials to support those changes Manage all program logistics independently: LMS administration, scheduling, roster management, Zoom coordination, and SME preparation and briefing Coordinate SME and supervisor involvement in training programs — providing clear direction and session context so their contributions are set up for impact Evaluate training effectiveness through assessment data, QA results, nesting observations, and supervisor feedback — and act on gaps proactively Develop and maintain coverage plans and program documentation so training continuity is never dependent on a single person's availability Collaborate with the broader Enablement team on knowledge management, L&D initiatives, and operational content projects Use AI-assisted tools to develop content efficiently, with full accountability for review and quality of all outputs What You'll Bring: 3+ years of experience in training, instructional design, or knowledge management, ideally within a contact center or customer service environment Bachelor's degree Demonstrated end-to-end ownership of training programs — you have built them, run them, and improved them without requiring step-by-step direction Strong project and time management skills, with the ability to manage multiple workstreams and deadlines concurrently Clear and proactive communication skills, with experience delivering training sessions virtually and in person Familiarity with knowledge management systems, learning management systems (LMS), or other documentation tools Collaborative mindset, with the ability to partner across teams and departments A proactive, ownership-driven approach to managing training programs and knowledge resources Knowledge of instructional design principles and adult learning methodologies Comfort with AI-assisted content development tools and strong editorial judgment about quality and accuracy of outputs Preferred not Required Experience: Experience designing or facilitating training in a contact center or call center environment — including soft skills, de-escalation, or member/customer communication training Health insurance industry experience Experience with Sana, Document360, or similar LMS and knowledge management platforms Why Join Us? Competitive salary ($65,000 - $75,000) + bonus opportunity and equity package Comprehensive Medical, Dental, and Vision benefits A 401k retirement plan Paid vacation and company holidays Opportunity to make an impact at a rapidly growing mission-driven company transforming healthcare in the U.S. Sidecar Health is an Equal Opportunity employer committed to building a diverse team. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status or disability status. Originally posted on Himalayas

job.title: Learning and Development Specialist
job.description: Who We Are Evergreen Nephrology partners with nephrologists to transform kidney care through a value-based, person-centered, holistic, and comprehensive approach to kidney care. We believe patients living with kidney disease deserve the best care. We are committed to improving patient outcomes and improving quality of life by delaying disease progression, shifting care to the home, and accelerating kidney transplants. We help nephrologists focus on the right patients at the right time across the full care spectrum. We do this by providing them with the best-in-class interdisciplinary clinical resources, analytical insight and tools, and services to patients. We listen to the needs of our patients, our employees, and our client partners, continually working to push beyond the status quo in which the care system manages patients today. Who You Are You are devoted, compassionate, and enjoy being on the front lines of healthcare, changing the lives of patients by supporting them and the team by focusing on customers. You’re excited about being part of a team that is building a healthcare delivery model that ensures the highest possible quality of life and best outcomes for those in our care. You believe people living with kidney disease deserve the best person-centered, holistic, comprehensive care and want to influence the healthcare system to drive towards that. You thrive in innovative and evolving environments with high rates of change. Your Role As a Learning and Development Specialist with Evergreen Nephrology , you are responsible for designing and developing high-impact training for our organization. You’ll partner with our clinical educator and other subject matter experts across all teams to create meaningful learning experiences for our team members. You will ensure that all learning designs are evidence-informed, scalable, and learner-centric. You will be the primary administrator for our Learning Management System, handling tasks such as uploading and testing learning content, overseeing assignments, and preparing reports. Role Responsibilities This role's primary duties include the following: Learning Design/Development: Conduct appropriate analyses to identify skill and knowledge gaps to improve performance on the job Oversee orientation and onboarding logistics and assist in facilitation of new hire onboarding Design, develop and manage annual compliance training processes in partnership with Legal & Compliance teams Collaborate with the Clinical Educator and other SMEs to design engaging and meaningful learning experiences for a variety of roles and clinical programs Serve as a thought partner to leaders and SMEs to influence learning design, rollout, and reinforcement strategies Design learning experiences for leadership development, role-based training, new team member onboarding, AI utilization, and other programs/initiatives, as needed Design and develop elearning courses, microlearning, and assessments Design and develop performance support materials (e.g. job aids, reference guides, toolkits) Leverage LinkedIn Learning to support ongoing education and training initiatives Measure training effectiveness through learner feedback, assessments, and performance outcomes Ensure learning content meets organizational, regulatory, and accreditation standards Partner with SMEs to regularly review and refresh content as roles, tools, or regulations evolve Support continuous improvement of all learning and development programs and initiatives Learning Systems Administration: Manage our current LMS (WorkRamp) end-to-end, including integration with our HRIS (Rippling), user permissions, assignments, and system settings Upload, test, and manage the lifecycle of training content Serve as the primary escalation point for LMS-related issues and system questions Ensure completion records are accurate for regulatory, safety, and accreditation requirements Utilize LMS tools and Microsoft Excel to generate, analyze, and interpret learning system data into meaningful dashboards and reports Manage LinkedIn Learning administration, including assignments, data analysis, and reporting Document LMS processes, workflows, and user guidance to support sustainability and knowledge transfer Monitor trends in participation, completion, assessment results, and learner engagement to inform improvements Manage LMS vendor relationship, including support tickets, enhancements, and roadmap discussions Other duties consistent with this role, as assigned Required Qualifications Bachelor's degree in education, Instructional Design, Organizational Development or related field 3-5 years of experience in instructional design in a business setting Minimum 1-year of experience with direct responsibility for LMS administration Intermediate skills with Articulate 360, specifically Storyline and Rise Intermediate skills with Snagit and Camtasia Studio Strong visual design skills Strong written and verbal communication skills Strong attention to detail Ability to create job-realistic learning scenarios Ability to identify and solve technical problems related to content, user access, or system errors Ability to organize, prioritize, and manage multiple projects Intermediate skills with MS Office Suite of products including Outlook and Teams A portfolio of work demonstrating your instructional design and visual design skills Able to work effectively in a primarily remote environment: Home internet must support a minimum download speed of 25 Mbps and upload speed of 10 Mbps. Cable, Fiber, or DSL connections hardwired to the internet device are recommended Evergreen will provide remote employees with telephony applications and equipment to meet the business requirements for their role Employees must work from a dedicated space lacking ongoing interruptions to protect member PHI / HIPAA information Preferred Qualifications Experience implementing Human Performance Improvement models Experience designing microlearning in 7taps Experience designing job aids, guides, etc. using Canva Experience with Articulate Reach Pro Experience with WorkRamp LMS Experience managing a LinkedIn Learning enterprise account Experience utilizing AI-enabled assistants and tools in your workflow Experience working in a healthcare setting Compensation The pay range for this role is $75,000 to $90,000 annually. Exact pay is determined based on experience, education, and other role-specific factors. This role is also eligible for an annual bonus. Benefits Evergreen Nephrology ’s total rewards program is designed to support you in and outside of work. You can expect: Paid time off starting at 4 weeks for full-time employees 12 paid holidays per year Medical, dental, vision and life insurance, including an HSA with employer match Reimbursement for continuing medical education for eligible roles A 401(k) program where Evergreen matches up to 4% of contributions after six months of tenure Paid parental leave A robust training and development program that starts with onboarding and continues throughout your career with Evergreen Nephrology Evergreen Nephrology is an equal opportunity employer. Applicants will not be discriminated against because of race, color, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, religion, national origin, citizenship status, disability, ancestry, marital status, veteran status, medical condition or any other protected category under local, state or federal laws. If you are an applicant with a disability who requires reasonable accommodation for any part of the hiring process, please contact us for assistance at talent@egneph.com . Originally posted on Himalayas

job.title: Customer Education Manager
job.description: Job Description: We believe in bold ideas, diverse perspectives, and the drive to transform knowledge into impact. Here, your curiosity fuels progress, your voice shapes innovation, and your ambition helps redefine what’s possible within science and learning. We are a culture that obsesses over impact, challenges, and drives what’s next to power infinite possibilities for our customers, colleagues and society at large. About the Role: As a key member of Wiley ’s Sales Operations organization, the Customer Education Manager plays an important role in supporting renewable revenue and advancing Wiley ’s customer‑first strategy. This role focuses on delivering high‑quality digital education experiences that help customers maximize the value of Wiley ’s products and agreements across institutional licensing, open access, and research workflows. This position is primarily responsible for developing and continuously improving two flagship digital learning platforms: Success Hub, Wiley ’s customer‑facing resource hub, and Wiley Academy, Wiley ’s LMS‑based learning platform. The ideal candidate is an experienced instructional designer and content creator who enjoys working in complex digital environments and collaborating across teams. Occasional support for live webinar delivery and APAC event operations may be required. How You Will Make an Impact Lead content development for Success Hub, including new content creation, content optimization, and decommissioning outdated materials Partner with UX and analytics teams to improve the hub experience and measure impact using tools such as Adobe Analytics Occasional support for live webinar delivery and APAC event operations may be required. Design and build short courses, learning paths, and certifications within Wiley Academy to drive product usage and customer value Create engaging customer‑facing training materials such as videos, guides, infographics, FAQs, and posts in collaboration with Sales, Product, CSMs, and Design teams Produce and edit high‑quality training videos using platforms such as Synthesia, Cloudinary, or equivalent tools Repurpose content across multiple formats and channels to maximize reach and effectiveness Support webinar production and facilitation (GoToWebinar/Zoom) for APAC customers as part of event operations Maintain organized, version‑controlled content libraries and documentation Collaborate cross‑functionally to align training content with customer needs, product priorities, and business goals Continuously improve training programs based on customer feedback and performance insights What We’re Looking For Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field 3+ years of experience in instructional design, e‑learning development, digital content creation, or customer education Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English, with experience creating polished, customer‑facing content Strong project and content management skills, with the ability to work across multiple priorities in a fast‑paced environment Experience working with Learning Management Systems (e.g., Skilljar or equivalent) and Content Management Systems (e.g., AEM, Webflow, or equivalent) Hands‑on experience with instructional design or authoring tools such as EasyGenerator, Articulate, or similar Experience with video creation or management tools is strongly preferred Comfortable collaborating across regions and time zones, and working with cross‑functional stakeholders Passion for education, customer success, and continuous improvement We power infinite possibilities. For more than 200 years, we've transformed knowledge into discoveries that shape the world. Today, our global team of innovators, creators, and experts is driving what's next in science, education, and publishing—creating impact that reaches everywhere. We're not just observers of progress. We're the ones accelerating scientific breakthroughs, advancing learning, and sparking innovation that redefines entire fields and improves lives. Here, your talent matters. Your ideas have room to grow. And your work creates breakthroughs that can change everything. Wiley is an equal opportunity/affirmative action employer. We evaluate all qualified applicants and treat all qualified applicants and employees without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, genetic information, or based on any individual's status in any group or class protected by applicable federal, state or local laws. Wiley is also committed to providing reasonable accommodation to applicants and employees with disabilities. Applicants who require accommodation to participate in the job application process may contact tasupport@wiley.com for assistance. We are proud that our workplace promotes continual learning and internal mobility. Our values support courageous teammates, needle movers, and learning champions all while striving to support the health and well-being of all employees. We offer meeting-free Friday afternoons allowing more time for heads down work and professional development, and through a robust body of employee programing we facilitate a wide range of opportunities to foster community, learn, and grow. We are committed to fair, transparent pay, and we strive to provide competitive compensation in addition to a comprehensive benefits package. It is anticipated that most qualified candidates will fall within the range, however the ultimate salary offered for this role may be higher or lower and will be set based on a variety of non-discriminatory factors, including but not limited to, geographic location, skills, and competencies. Wiley proactively displays target base pay range for United Kingdom, Canada and USA based roles. When applying, please attach your resume/CV to be considered. Job Posting Title: Customer Education Manager Location: Remote, New Delhi, IND Originally posted on Himalayas

match_snapshot.ai_reasoning: Anil's 18+ years in L&D curriculum design, LMS configuration, and product management at Thomson Reuters aligns almost perfectly with Wiley's need for an instructional designer managing LMS-based learning platforms. His direct experience building and managing product catalogs, LMS design and implementation, and digital learning content creation mirrors the Success Hub and Wiley Academy mandate. The publishing/knowledge platform domain (Thomson Reuters Knowledge Platform) also signals immediate cultural and business-model fit with Wiley.

follow_up_messages.content:
Thank you for taking the time to speak with me today about the Pre-Sales Solutions Engineer role.

The conversation reinforced why this opportunity genuinely excites me — particularly the positioning at the intersection of sales, engineering, and solution architecture. That's exactly where I've operated for 25 years, most recently at AMD India where I've been bridging technical depth with commercial outcomes across OEM partnerships and government channels.

What stood out to me was the customer-facing nature of the role and the autonomy it offers. I thrive in environments where I'm trusted to own the technical narrative in front of clients, and I believe my background in enterprise solution selling and GeM marketplace positioning translates directly here.

I'm enthusiastic about contributing to your India region growth and would welcome any follow-up questions. Looking forward to hearing about the next steps.

Warm regards,
Anirban Dey

Thank you for taking the time to speak with me today about the Pre-Sales Solutions Engineer role at Gateway Search.

The conversation reinforced my excitement about this position — particularly the way it sits at the intersection of sales, engineering, and solution architecture. That's exactly the space I've operated in throughout my career, and where I do my best work.

After 25 years bridging technical depth with commercial outcomes — from OEM partnerships with HP, Dell, and Lenovo to navigating complex government procurement cycles — I'm confident I can bring immediate value to your international client in the India region.

I genuinely enjoyed our discussion and would welcome the opportunity to move forward. Please don't hesitate to reach out if you need anything further from my end.

Looking forward to hearing from you.

Warm regards,
Anirban Dey

Before submitting my application, I wanted to reach out directly — the customer-facing intersection of sales, engineering, and solution architecture described in this role is essentially where I've spent the last decade.

At AMD India, I've led pre-sales and field engineering efforts across OEM partnerships with HP, Dell, Lenovo, and Acer, while also navigating government procurement channels including GeM. That combination of technical depth and strategic solution positioning across diverse stakeholders is something I genuinely enjoy — and it maps closely to what this role demands.

What caught my attention specifically is the India-region ownership this position carries. Having operated across enterprise and public sector accounts here for 25 years, I bring both the relationships and the ground-level market understanding to contribute from day one.

I'd welcome a brief conversation if you think there's a fit worth exploring.

Warm regards,
Anirban Dey

Anirban Dey

Thank you for taking the time to meet with me today — I genuinely enjoyed the conversation.

Anirban Dey

job.title: Technology Field Application Engineer
job.description: Position: Technology Field Application Engineer Job Description: Principal Accountabilities Be part of driving technical sales results for the embedded microcontroller and microprocessor team, in particular, championing wireless connectivity. Actively drive customer discovery, promoting technologies, technical support through customer site visits, conference calls, emails, support platforms. Responsible for utilizing an advanced understanding of the customer needs and technical leadership expertise to champion return on engineering investment. ● Drive demand creation by maximizing Arrow content on designs. Has an advanced understanding of the customer’s needs and leverage Arrow’s engineering and design services resources to identify & facilitate solutions for customers. Apply intermediate technical expertise to influence designs utilizing solution selling, resources, and strategies. ● Partner with the sales team across pre-and post-sales activities, providing more advanced technical support and consulting to promote demand creation. Perform and drive technical calibrations & research with sales, other Arrow departments, and suppliers in support of the customer design. Champion return on engineering investment, design win priorities, initiatives, and promotions by obtaining buy-in and support from sales. ● Attend supplier trainings in person and via conference calls. This may involve foreign travel on occasions. Demonstrate an intermediate understanding of the strengths and weaknesses of supplier technologies and how those technologies relate to market needs. Manage supplier and customer expectations by building and developing relationships, keeping stakeholders up-to-date on activities, plans, and progressing towards initiatives. Deepen and broaden connections with key decision makers within suppliers and customers. ● Strategic role: Attend and actively participate in strategic branch meetings and QBR. Develop and drive strategic technical plans by supplier line or customer solutions. Participate in ArrowEDGE initiatives through branch training and customer calls. job.title: EMEA OEM Sales Manager
job.description: OEM Alliance Manager, EMEA Focus: NVIDIA | Dell | Supermicro | Hitachi | HPE | Lenovo WEKA is architecting a new approach to the enterprise data stack built for the AI era. The WEKA® Data Platform sets the standard for AI infrastructure with a cloud and AI-native software solution that can be deployed anywhere. It transforms legacy data silos into data pipelines that make GPUs, AI, and other performance-intensive workloads run faster, work more efficiently, and consume less energy. WEKA is a pre-IPO, growth-stage company on a hyper-growth trajectory. We've raised $375M in capital with dozens of world-class venture capital and strategic investors. We help the world's most innovative enterprises and research organizations, including 12 of the Fortune 50, reach discoveries, insights, and outcomes faster and more sustainably. We're passionate about helping our customers overcome complex data challenges to accelerate their innovation and help grow their businesses. If you share our passion, we invite you to join us on this exciting journey. What's Cool About This Job If you are a seasoned alliance professional eager to make a lasting impact across the world's leading OEM and technology partners, WEKA invites you to join our team and shape the future of AI infrastructure in EMEA. Bring Your Expertise – and Your Passion WEKA seeks a dynamic and experienced OEM Alliance Manager to spearhead our growth across key hardware and technology partnerships in the EMEA region. This pivotal role spans our most strategic OEM relationships — NVIDIA, Dell, Supermicro, Hitachi, HPE, and Lenovo — and calls for a strategic thinker, a relationship builder, and a passionate advocate for innovative AI infrastructure solutions. As the primary owner of these alliances in EMEA, you will align WEKA's platform with the go-to-market motions of the world's leading compute, storage, and AI hardware vendors, driving joint pipeline, enablement, and revenue growth across the region. Responsibilities Forge strategic alliances: Cultivate deep partnerships with sales, technical, and alliance teams across NVIDIA, Dell, Supermicro, Hitachi, HPE, and Lenovo — empowering them with the tools and knowledge to drive customer success with WEKA. Lead with: Develop focused go-to-market strategies that position WEKA as the data platform of choice within NVIDIA's AI ecosystem and the OEM’s product portfolios, leveraging the breadth and reach of both organizations across EMEA. Elevate AI workloads: Build out a dedicated alliance motion to capitalize on surging demand for GPU-optimized AI servers, positioning WEKA as the premier data platform across AI infrastructure deployments. Drive business growth: Develop and execute a multi-partner channel development plan that aligns with each OEM's regional growth objectives and joint business priorities. Build bridges: Secure executive-level buy-in and foster collaboration across diverse OEM teams to create compelling joint value propositions tailored to each partner's customer base and market motion. Enable and empower: Organize comprehensive training programs to equip OEM sales and pre-sales teams across all partner organizations with the skills and insights they need to excel in positioning WEKA. Nurture relationships: Build trust and rapport with partner sales teams across all six OEMs, positioning yourself as a go-to resource, trusted advisor, and champion of joint opportunities. Coordinate across partners: Identify and develop cross-OEM opportunities — particularly where NVIDIA accelerated compute, Dell or Supermicro infrastructure, and Hitachi, HPE, or Lenovo platforms intersect — to create differentiated, integrated solutions for customers. Desired Qualifications Deep industry expertise: At least 10 years of experience in technology vendor sales, with a proven track record in channel and OEM business development. Prior experience in storage, AI infrastructure, or HPC is a significant advantage. NVIDIA and Dell acumen: Hands-on experience working with NVIDIA's partner ecosystem — including DGX, HGX, or AI Enterprise programs — and Dell's enterprise sales and alliance ecosystem. A strong understanding of how these two partners intersect in AI infrastructure deployments is highly desirable. Supermicro proficiency: Familiarity with Supermicro's GPU server and AI infrastructure portfolio and an ability to build and scale an alliance motion with a fast-moving, engineering-led OEM. Broader OEM fluency: Familiarity with HPE's partner and alliance programs, Hitachi's enterprise solutions business, and Lenovo's Infrastructure Solutions Group (ISG) channel motion in EMEA. Relationship-building prowess: A knack for forging strong connections and building consensus among diverse stakeholders across multiple organizations simultaneously. Strategic thinking: A keen eye for identifying opportunities across a complex, multi-partner landscape and developing effective plans to capitalize on them. Excellent communication skills: The ability to articulate complex ideas clearly and persuasively, both verbally and in writing, across cultures and organizational levels. Concerned you don't meet every qualification? Don't let it stop you from applying! WEKA is committed to building a diverse, inclusive, and authentic workplace. If you are excited about this position but don't meet every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway – you may just be the right candidate for this or other positions at WEKA. WEKA is an equal-opportunity employer that prohibits discrimination and harassment of any kind. We provide equal opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation, and training. Originally posted on Himalayas

The main gap is EMEA geography and storage/AI infrastructure domain knowledge, but his OEM alliance playbook is directly transferable.

job.title: Business Development Manager, Global Public Sector
job.description: Reporting to the Senior Director of Global Public Sector, you will drive the growth for NVIDIA ’s platform across LATAM region in public sector, in support of the overall go-to-market (GTM) strategy. You will be instrumental in growing NVIDIA ’s business by implementing our national plans following a programmatic approach to growing revenue. In this role, you will collaborate with our field sales organization, solution architects, and developer relations teams. You'll lead engagements with governments, regulators, NVIDIA cloud partners, and ecosystem partners to advance national AI clouds, align on policy and compliance, and deploy secure and resilient infrastructure. You will also drive local ecosystem growth by connecting public sector, startups, ISVs, GSIs, and academia, while supporting workforce development to operationalize AI at scale. What you'll be doing: Drive the execution of the worldwide public sector GTM strategy and business plan in collaboration with regional Sales and Marketing to drive mutual success Lead country-level strategies for sovereign AI, ensuring alignment with national data residency and security requirements while accelerating adoption of NVIDIA platform throughout model building and application stages. Promote end-to-end AI lifecycle capabilities (data curation, training, fine-tuning, inference, and deployment) with a strong emphasis on NVIDIA platforms such as NeMo/Nemotron, TensorRT, and infrastructure. Enable public sector industry adoption by translating compliance, governance, and privacy requirements into deployable NVIDIA -based AI architectures. Develop Light House Accounts by identifying partners and government agencies that are at the forefront of AI. Track and progress the various engagements with a goal to grow GPU adoption and customers’ wallet share in NVIDIA platform. We make heavy use of conferencing tools, but some travel is required (20%) What we need to see: Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience or an equivalent level of professional background from a leading University. Master’s degree or equivalent experience, MBA is desirable 5+ years of business development experience in hi-tech selling technology solutions Confirmed grasp of AI and the AI ecosystem; eager to deepen expertise Strong analytical skills, including the ability to conduct market assessments Excellent written and verbal communication skills in both Spanish and English Proven ability to understand and articulate key product features, messaging, and positioning Experience enabling local AI ecosystems—developers, startups, academia, and the public sector—through AI tooling and infrastructure Ways to stand out from the crowd: Deep knowledge of AI and NVIDIA AI stack (NeMo, Nemotron, CUDA, TensorRT, NIMs) across both training and inference stages. Proven experience building or scaling national AI initiatives Strong understanding of AI model lifecycle economics, including GPU utilization, training vs. inference cost tradeoffs, and optimization strategies. Knowledge of Generative AI, combined with deep understanding of government operations and local AI landscape in the target market. Proven track record in presenting sophisticated technical content, while clearly communicating the value and return on investment of investments in AI. NVIDIA is widely considered one of the technology world’s most desirable employers. We have some of the world's most forward-thinking and hardworking people on our team. If you're creative and autonomous, we want to hear from you! Your base salary will be determined based on your location, experience, and the pay of employees in similar positions. The base salary range is 128,000 USD - 201,250 USD for Level 3, and 152,000 USD - 247,250 USD for Level 4. You will also be eligible for equity and benefits . Applications for this job will be accepted at least until May 10, 2026. This posting is for an existing vacancy. NVIDIA uses AI tools in its recruiting processes. NVIDIA is committed to fostering a diverse work environment and proud to be an equal opportunity employer. As we highly value diversity in our current and future employees, we do not discriminate (including in our hiring and promotion practices) on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, disability status or any other characteristic protected by law. job.title: Field Applications Engineer (FAE)
job.description: Job Details: Job Description: For decades, Altera has been at the forefront of programmable logic technology. Our commitment to innovation has empowered countless customers to create groundbreaking solutions that have transformed industries. Join us in our journey to becoming the #1 FPGA company! What You Will Do As a Field Applications Engineer (FAE) , your key responsibilities will be: Driving FPGA Adoption: Identify, influence, and enable the adoption of Altera FPGA-based solutions at key customer accounts. Collaboration & Teamwork: Work effectively within a cross-functional team, including Field Sales Engineers, other FAEs, Factory Applications Engineers, Product Marketing, and Business Development. Relationship Building: Cultivate strong relationships with customer engineering teams to identify, develop, and expand new opportunities within assigned accounts. Product Expertise: Possess deep knowledge of the FPGA product portfolio to drive sales in established product lines and seed growth in emerging markets. Solution Positioning: Articulate the value proposition of Altera FPGAs, demonstrating how they provide optimal solutions for specific customer applications. Technical Assistance: Assist customers with: architecture definition, solution evaluation, design and implementation of product requirements using FPGAs, IP, and development tools. Technical Demonstrations & Support: d evelop and deliver compelling product demonstrations, participate in on-site customer visits to assist with product design and reviews, ensure customer requirements are met throughout the design process. Customer Issue Resolution: Build strong relationships with customer teams to facilitate rapid resolution of technical issues and ensure high levels of customer satisfaction. Problem Solving & Analysis: Analyze data to diagnose problems, reproduce errors, and resolve or escalate customer issues effectively. Feedback & Product Improvement: Provide timely feedback to manufacturing and product teams regarding product issues, future requirements, and market trends to ensure Altera remains responsive and proactive.
